name: Sierra de la Demanda – circular from Ezcaray por Valdezcaray
name_original: Sierra de la Demanda – circular desde Ezcaray por Valdezcaray
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ezcaray are situated at the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, offering diverse landscapes. The region is characterized by the Oja River valley, featuring Pyrenean oak forests, poplar groves, and cultivated fields that transition into beech forests at higher elevations. Impressive limestone cliffs and rolling hills also define the terrain, providing varied scenery for cyclists. This area offers a mix of challenging mountain passes and accessible greenways. Best touring cycling routes around Ezcaray The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de la Demanda – circular from Ezcaray por Valdezcaray, a 40.9 miles (65.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the Sierra de la Demanda, including the Valdezcaray area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bonicaparra Shelter – Oja Greenway loop from Ezcaray, a difficult 22.5 miles (36.3 km) path. This route incorporates sections of the Oja Greenway, a scenic path through the Oja Valley with varied ecosystems. Local touring cyclists also love the Beech Forest of GR 190 – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from Ezcaray, a 11.3 miles (18.1 km) trail leading through beech forests, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Ezcaray is defined by the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, the Oja River valley, and dense beech and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain climbs to more accessible valley routes. The routes in Ezcaray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 23 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ezcaray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ezcaray?
A: Ezcaray offers a wide range of touring cycling routes, with over 40 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from challenging mountain passes to more accessible valley paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ezcaray?
A: The terrain around Ezcaray is highly diverse. You'll find routes traversing the dramatic Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, picturesque sections through the Oja River valley with Pyrenean oak and poplar groves, and ascents into dense beech forests. The landscape also features impressive limestone cliffs and rolling hills, providing varied scenery for cyclists.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options suitable for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oja Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers a predominantly paved or compacted gravel path through the Oja Valley, making it more accessible. There are 2 easy routes and 8 moderate routes available, providing gentler gradients and shorter distances.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Ezcaray?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge will find plenty of options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994466669/sierra-de-la-demanda-circular-from-ezcaray-por-valdezcaray" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda – circular from Ezcaray por Valdezcaray&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 40.9-mile (65.9 km) trail with significant elevation gain, including the climb to Valdezcaray, which has been featured in the Vuelta a España. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Shelter – Oja Greenway loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 22.5-mile (36.3 km) path with over 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ezcaray?
A: The summer months are particularly popular for outdoor activities in Ezcaray, including touring cycling. During this time, the Valdezcaray ski resort area, typically a winter destination, transforms into a high-mountain landscape ideal for cycling. Spring and early autumn also offer pleasant conditions with vibrant foliage and comfortable temperatures.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Ezcaray?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Ezcaray are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994466669/sierra-de-la-demanda-circular-from-ezcaray-por-valdezcaray" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda – circular from Ezcaray por Valdezcaray&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Shelter – Oja Greenway loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beech Forest of GR 190 – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Ezcaray?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the Sierra de la Demanda and the Oja River valley, you can explore various points of interest. Routes may pass by shelters like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3266568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Shelter&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4479300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Prao Tajo&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4598570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt; or the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2918989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán de la Cogolla Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, known for its Suso and Yuso monasteries.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on Ezcaray bike routes?
A: Absolutely. The region's diverse landscape, from the Oja River valley to the Sierra de la Demanda, offers numerous scenic vistas. The climb towards Valdezcaray provides stunning mountain views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4539513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pradilla&lt;/a&gt; is another notable mountain pass offering panoramic scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Ezcaray?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ezcaray are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 23 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's varied terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to accessible greenways, and the beautiful natural settings, including dense beech and oak forests and the picturesque Oja River valley.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks in Ezcaray itself that cyclists can visit?
A: Yes, Ezcaray's charming town center is worth exploring. It features traditional architecture, arcaded squares, emblazoned palaces, and Gothic churches like Santa María la Mayor. These cultural elements provide a pleasant respite and additional points of interest for visitors exploring the region by bike.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the Oja Greenway?
A: Yes, the Oja Greenway is a significant feature for touring cyclists in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19882820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Shelter – Oja Greenway loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that incorporates sections of this scenic path, which follows a repurposed old railway line through the Oja Valley.
tips: Ski station at a minimum elevation of 1,530 meters and a maximum elevation of 2,125 meters. Located in the Sierra de la Demanda of La Rioja, the station uses the slopes of the San Lorenzo peak.
Impressive viewpoint near the peak of La Rasilla, at an altitude of 1,874 meters. You can see the beautiful views of much of the Sierra de la Demanda.

It has been the end of a stage in the Vuelta a España on occasion.
Mythical port of the Sierra de la Demanda with privileged views over the valley and the mountain ranges.

Temple of Romanesque origin located in the town of Ezcaray, built during the 12th century and rebuilt in the Gothic style between the 14th and 16th centuries. The building is built with reddish stone typical of the Sierra de la Demanda.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María la Mayor of Ezcaray
